data.title: Asset Entities regains compliance with Nasdaq
data.description:
• Asset Entities (NASDAQ:ASST) said it has regained compliance with Nasdaq's rule, which requires minimum stockholders’ equity of $2.5 million.
  • Press Release

data.title: DocGo expands into Chattanooga region in collaboration with Tennessee healthcare system
data.description:
• DocGo (NASDAQ:DCGO) said on Wednesday that its Ambulnz subsidiary has extended its contract with a major Tennessee healthcare system to launch services in Chattanooga, TN.
  • Ambulnz by DocGo will provide vital services in the Chattanooga region, including transportation for hospital discharges and transfers among skilled nursing, rehabilitation and acute care facilities.
  • The Tennessee partnership supports approximately 17,000 annual trips, with plans to exceed 20,000 trips per year in 2025. This expansion is projected to add 5,000 additional trips in the state by 2026.
  • In the past year alone, the company increased the number of trips in Tennessee over 25%, reflecting a growing demand for these services.
  • DocGo (NASDAQ:DCGO) shares were up 2.32% in pre-market trading.

data.title: PHX Energy appoints new CEO
data.description:
• PHX Energy Services (TSX:PHX:CA) announced that John Hooks, the founder and current Chief Executive Officer will be transitioning to Executive Board Chair effective March 1, 2025, and its Board of Directors has appointed, Michael Buker, currently the President, as his CEO successor.
  • In addition to assuming the role of President and CEO, Buker is expected to be nominated for election to the Board of Directors at the Corporation’s next Annual General Meeting of Shareholders.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Shoppers spent less at Etsy during holidays
data.description:
Shoppers spent less at Etsy (NASDAQ:ETSY) during the holiday season, driving down gross merchandise sales (“GMS”) and leaving Wall Street disappointed with the online marketplace’s fourth quarter results and expectations for the current quarter.

Shares were down 5% ahead of Wednesday’s opening bell.

“Despite facing significant GMS headwinds in 2024, we are proud to have delivered year-over-year revenue growth and very strong profitability, while simultaneously investing in our future," said CEO Josh Silverman.

Although the company reactivated 9.8M lapsed buyers from last year, spending on the platform was down 6.8% overall and the number of active buyers fell 2.6%. Spending per buyer was also down, dropping 3.5% from last year to $121, while the number of habitual buyers fell by 9.5% to 6.4M.

But while marketplace revenue was down 1.4%, services revenue increased 8.1%, driving the company’s total revenue to $852.2B, a 1.2% increase from last year but $9.2M less than expected. Profits also improved thanks to a 15% improvement in cost of sales and beat expectations by 9 cents.

Looking ahead to the current quarter, Etsy (NASDAQ:ETSY) continues to anticipate anemic GMS, declining “at a rate similar to the year-over-year performance for the fourth quarter.”

data.title: Electric truck maker Nikola files for Chapter 11 bankruptcy after embattled journey
data.description:
Nikola Corporation (NASDAQ:NKLA) plunged in early trading on Wednesday after the company announced that it filed voluntary petitions under Chapter 11 of the Bankruptcy Code in the United States Bankruptcy Court for the District of Delaware. Nikola (NASDAQ:NKLA) said it has also filed a motion seeking authorization to pursue an auction and sale process under Section 363 of the U.S. Bankruptcy Code.

Subject to court approval, Nikola (NKLA) intends to continue certain limited directly provided service and support operations for trucks currently in the field, including certain HYLA fueling operations through the end of March. Thereafter, the company will need one or more partners to support such activities.

Nikola (NKLA) cruises into Chapter 11 with approximately $47 million in cash on hand to fund the foregoing activities, implement the post-petition sale process, and exit Chapter 11 through a plan process. Given the company's liquidity profile and the anticipated expense of the cases and limited operations in Chapter 11, the company intends to request authority from the court to consummate a sale of its assets on a timeline that balances the liquidity needs.

CEO statement: "Like other companies in the electric vehicle industry, we have faced various market and macroeconomic factors that have impacted our ability to operate. In recent months, we have taken numerous actions to raise capital, reduce our liabilities, clean up our balance sheet and preserve cash to sustain our operations. Unfortunately, our very best efforts have not been enough to overcome these significant challenges, and the Board has determined that Chapter 11 represents the best possible path forward under the circumstances for the Company and its stakeholders."

Nikola (NKLA) was founded in 2014 by Trevor Milton in Salt Lake City, Utah. The company set a goal to revolutionize the transportation industry with a focus on zero-emission vehicles. In its early years, Nikola (NKLA) garnered significant investor attention with the unveiling of its first hydrogen-electric semi-truck prototype, the Nikola One, in 2016.

Nikola (NKLA) went public on June 4, 2020, through a merger with VectoIQ Acquisition Corp. However, Nikola (NKLA) soon faced serious allegations of fraud, leading to investigations by regulatory bodies and the resignation of Trevor Milton as Executive Chairman.

data.title: Core Scientific expands into Alabama with new computing facility
data.description:
• Core Scientific (NASDAQ:CORZ) announced on Wednesday its expansion into Auburn, Alabama, with a new high-performance computing facility at 1571 W Samford Ave.
  • The Core Scientific Auburn data center, housed within the existing AUBix facility, will launch with a capacity of 16 megawatts and has potential for further expansion.
  • Core Scientific is planning to make an initial investment of approximately $135M in the facility, with total investment in the project expected to exceed $400M, including substantial capital expenditures from future partners.
  • In the process of becoming the operator of the Samford Avenue facility, Core Scientific has leased the building and is planning to execute an option to purchase.
  • It has retained the facility’s 11 full-time employees as part of its commitment to the local workforce. The company plans to increase its team to a minimum of 20 full-time employees.
  • Core Scientific (NASDAQ:CORZ) shares were up 0.79% in pre-market trading.

data.title: Wingstop reports mixed Q4 earnings; initiates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Wingstop press release (NASDAQ:WING): Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.92 beats by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $161.8M (+27.3% Y/Y) misses by $2.78M.
  • System-wide sales increased 27.6% to $1.2 billion
  • 105 net new openings in the fiscal fourth quarter 2024
  • Domestic restaurant AUV increased to $2.1 million
  • Domestic same store sales increased 10.1%
  • Digital sales increased to 70.3% of system-wide sales
  • Adjusted EBITDA, a non-GAAP measure, increased 44.2% to $56.3 million

  • Financial Outlook

    The Company expects the following for fiscal year 2025:

    • Low- to mid-single digit domestic same store sales growth;
    • Global unit growth rate of 14% to 15%;
    • SG&A of approximately $140 million, which includes system implementation costs of approximately $4.5 million;
    • Stock-based compensation expense of approximately $26 million;
    • Interest expense, net of approximately $46 million; and
    • Depreciation and amortization of between $29 - $30 million.

data.title: Diversified Energy to launch 8.5M-share public offering
data.description:
Diversified Energy (OTCQX:DEC) -4.4% pre-market Wednesday after saying it launched a U.S. public offering of as much as 8.5M common shares, with an underwriters option to purchase up to an additional 850K shares at the public offering price.

The company plans to use the net proceeds to repay part of the debt expected to be incurred in connection with the proposed acquisition of Maverick Natural Resources.

Last month, Diversified Energy (OTCQX:DEC) announced the acquisition of Maverick Natural Resources for $1.275B including debt, expanding the company into the Permian Basin.

The two companies' "combined footprint in Oklahoma and the Western Anadarko Basin creates one of the largest in terms of production and acreage, which includes the emerging Cherokee formation," Diversified (OTCQX:DEC) CEO Rusty Hutson said.
